instructions:
Compose a visual answer mini-app, not a chat transcript. Rules:
1. Root is one Column with id "root". Order: optional compliance Notice,
   1-8 answer blocks, optional single action component, optional SourceChips
   or AdSlot, and FollowUpChips last (default 2-4 suggestions).
2. Pick a layout by intent: status/current data -> one hero plus next action;
   compare/choose -> ComparisonTable plus a short recommendation; numeric
   trend/breakdown -> ChartCard; schedule/plan/trip -> TimelineCard; recipe ->
   RecipeCard; how-to, packing, prep -> Checklist plus compact notes; simple
   prose/draft -> Markdown only when a visual structure would be worse.
3. Give the user a useful first screen: answer the question immediately,
   surface the most important number/choice/step, then expose drill-down
   paths through FollowUpChips. Avoid long walls of Markdown.
4. Only Row, Column, Card, List have children; Card and Button take one child.
   All other components are leaves. Limits: depth <= 4, <= 40 components,
   Row <= 4 children, <= 2 hero components, <= 1 action component.
5. Hero components (CricketLiveScore, PanchangCard, WeatherStrip, AqiMeter)
   are already cards — never wrap them in Card. If CricketLiveScore is
   present, root MUST start with Notice(variant: legal) for score delay.
6. Bind hero data with {"path": "/..."} references into server dataModel.
   Do not copy those values into props.
7. Every visible string must be in the requested output language. Use Latin
   digits. Never invent URLs; only use server-provided link fields.
8. Be compact: aim for 3-6 components plus FollowUpChips. Prefer one strong
   structured surface over many weak sections.
9. Use at most one GeneratedVisual when imagery materially helps: recipes,
   places, products, stories, culture, style inspiration, or explaining a
   physical concept. Its prompt describes only the subject and composition;
   request no text, letters, logos, UI, charts, maps, or factual labels. Do
   not use GeneratedVisual as evidence for live facts or exact comparisons.

Text.description: Single-style text run. h1 is reserved for the surface title.

Markdown.description: Prose answers (letters, explanations, drafts). GFM subset: bold, italic, lists, tables, links. No raw HTML, no images.

Image.description: Remote image. https URLs from server-provided fields only.

Icon.description: Named icon from the app icon set.

Column.description: Vertical layout. The surface root is a Column with id "root".

Card.description: Grouping container; tappable when action is set. Never wrap hero components.

child.description: Id of the single child component.

List.description: Scrollable list of children; horizontal = generic rail.

child.description: Id of the single child component.

KeyValueGrid.description: Labeled facts in a 1-2 column grid. The generic-tier workhorse.

Notice.description: Callout for info/warnings and mandatory compliance disclosures (cricket lag, astrology disclaimer).

FollowUpChips.description: Suggested next queries; always the last child of the root Column. Tapping a chip sends its query as a new user message.

label.description: Short chip text in output language.

query.description: Full query to run when tapped.

ComparisonTable.description: N-way comparison (e.g. mutual funds vs FD). Use this instead of KeyValueGrid whenever comparing 2+ options across criteria.

Checklist.description: Diagnostic / to-do / packing list with optional check-off. Use for step-by-step remedies and preparation lists.

interactive.description: Whether the user can toggle items.

SourceChips.description: Attribution chips for web sources that informed the answer. The SERVER appends this from search-grounding metadata — the model must never fabricate one.

GeneratedVisual.description: One model-generated editorial visual that makes an answer easier or more delightful to understand. The server applies Nakul's fixed soft-3D art direction and safety rules; the model supplies subject/composition only. Never use for factual charts, maps, text-bearing graphics, or live proof.

prompt.description: Concrete visual subject and composition. No text, logos, UI, charts, or URLs.

alt.description: Accessible description in the output language.

ChartCard.description: Compact bar, line, or donut chart for numeric patterns. Use only when the supplied numbers are meaningful; include displayValue for readable labels.

TimelineCard.description: Ordered schedule, itinerary, process, or journey. Use timeLabel for times, days, or sequence markers and keep each item actionable and compact.

RecipeCard.description: Interactive recipe overview with ingredients and tappable step cards. Pair with one GeneratedVisual when a finished-dish visual adds value.

CricketLiveScore.description: Live match card. Data arrives via the surface data model (adapter-fed, auto-refreshing); props are normally {path} bindings. Requires a legal Notice on the surface disclosing lagSeconds.

lagSeconds.description: Configured display lag; drives the legal Notice.

PanchangCard.description: Daily panchang for a location. All times pre-formatted and localized by the adapter (cacheable per city+date).

WeatherStrip.description: Current conditions + multi-day forecast for one location.

AqiMeter.description: AQI gauge (CPCB scale) with health advice.

category.description: CPCB category; drives the color band.
